setup.traces.title: Time to log your first trace, it only takes a minute
setup.traces.description: Get your API keys first, then ask your coding agent to add observability with Langfuse to your application.
setup.traces.step.create-api-keys.title: Create API keys
setup.traces.step.create-api-keys.descri: Your application needs API keys to send traces to Langfuse.
setup.traces.step.agent.title: Add tracing with your coding agent
setup.traces.step.agent.description: Paste this prompt into Claude, Cursor, Copilot, or another coding agent.
setup.traces.agent.prompt: Install the Langfuse AI skill from github.com/langfuse/skills and use it to add tracing to this application with Langfuse following best practices.
setup.traces.manual-docs-link: or follow our docs to set up tracing manually
setup.traces.step.run-app.title: Run your app — traces will appear here
setup.traces.step.run-app.description: Once your app makes an LLM call, traces show up within seconds.
layout.project-not-found.title: Project Not Found
layout.project-not-found.message: The project you are trying to access does not exist or you do not have access to it.
layout.drawer.support-description: A list of resources and options to help you with your questions.
organization.demo.title: Try Langfuse Demo
organization.demo.description: We have built a Q&A chatbot that answers questions based on the Langfuse Docs. Interact with it to see traces in Langfuse.
organization.overview.help: Organizations help you manage access to projects. Each organization can have multiple projects and team members with different roles.
organization.onboarding.can-create: Create an organization to get started. Alternatively, ask your organization admin to invite you.
organization.onboarding.need-invite: You need to get invited to an organization to get started with Langfuse.
organization.name.title: Organization Name
organization.rename.preview: Your Organization will be renamed from "{currentName}" to "{nextName}".
ai-features.title: AI Features
ai-features.enable-title: Enable AI powered features for your organization
ai-features.enable-description-before: This setting applies to all users and projects. Any data
ai-features.enable-description-after: be sent to AWS Bedrock within the Langfuse data region. Traces are sent to Langfuse Cloud in your data region. Your data will not be used for training models. Applicable HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR, and ISO 27001 compliance remains intact.
ai-features.more-details: More details in the docs here.
ai-features.telemetry-title: AI Data Use for Product/Service Improvement
ai-features.telemetry-description: Share data about your use of AI with Langfuse for product and service improvement.
ai-features.confirm-after: AI features for your organization. When enabled, any data
ai-features.confirm-processing: be sent to AWS Bedrock in your data region for processing.
ai-features.proceed: Are you sure you want to proceed?
organization.delete.toast.title: Organization Deleted
organization.delete.toast.description: The organization has been successfully deleted.
organization.delete.title: Delete Organization
organization.delete.has-projects: You can only delete an organization if it has no projects associated with it. Please delete or transfer all projects first. Deleting projects may take a few minutes.
organization.delete.confirm-input: To confirm, type "{confirmMessage}" in the input box
ai-features.disabled.ask-admin: Ask your organization administrator to enable AI features in organization settings.
setup.title: Setup
setup.description: Create a new organization. This will be used to manage your projects and teams.
setup.organization-description: Organizations are used to manage your projects and teams.
setup.project-description: Projects are used to group traces, datasets, evals and prompts. Environments can be separated using the built-in environment feature.
users.help.description-before: Attribute data in Langfuse to a user by adding a userId to your traces. See
users.table.user-id-description: The unique identifier for the user that was logged in Langfuse. See docs for more details on how to set this up.
users.table.first-event-description: The earliest trace recorded for this user.
users.table.last-event-description: The latest trace recorded for this user.
users.table.total-events-description: Total number of events for the user, includes traces and observations. See data model for more details.
users.table.total-tokens-description: Total number of tokens used for the user across all generations.
users.table.total-cost-description: Total cost for the user across all generations.
onboarding.prompts.value.decoupled.title: Decoupled from code
onboarding.prompts.value.decoupled.descr: Deploy new prompts without application redeployment, making updates faster and easier
onboarding.prompts.value.edit.title: Edit in UI or programmatically
onboarding.prompts.value.edit.descriptio: Non-technical users can easily edit prompts in the UI. Developers can optionally update prompts programmatically via the API and SDKs
onboarding.prompts.value.performance.des: Client-side caching prevents latency or availability issues for your applications
onboarding.prompts.value.metrics.title: Compare metrics
onboarding.prompts.value.metrics.descrip: Track latency, cost, and evaluation metrics across different prompt versions
onboarding.prompts.title: Get Started with Prompt Management
onboarding.prompts.description: Langfuse Prompt Management helps you centrally manage, version control, and collaboratively iterate on your prompts. Start using prompt management to improve your LLM application's performance and maintainability.
onboarding.evaluators.value.automate.des: Use LLM-as-a-judge to automatically evaluate your traces without manual review
onboarding.evaluators.value.quality.desc: Create custom evaluation criteria to measure the quality of your LLM outputs
onboarding.evaluators.value.scale.title: Scale efficiently
onboarding.evaluators.value.scale.descri: Evaluate thousands of traces automatically with customizable sampling rates
Monitor evaluation metrics over time to identify trends and improvements
onboarding.evaluators.title: Get started with evaluations
onboarding.evaluators.description-before: Use evaluators to score traces and observations automatically. Langfuse supports two evaluator types:
onboarding.evaluators.llm-description: use an LLM to score outputs against natural-language criteria.
onboarding.evaluators.code-description: use {language} logic for deterministic, custom scoring.
onboarding.evaluators.llm-judge-descript: Create evaluation templates and evaluators to automatically score your traces with LLM-as-a-judge. Set up custom evaluation criteria and let AI help you measure the quality of your outputs.
onboarding.annotation.value.workflows.de: Create and manage annotation queues to streamline your scoring workflows
Invite team members to annotate and evaluate your LLM outputs
onboarding.annotation.value.metrics.desc: Monitor annotation progress and quality metrics across your team
onboarding.annotation.value.baseline.des: Use annotation data as a baseline to evaluate your other evaluation metrics
onboarding.annotation.title: Get Started with Annotation Queues
onboarding.annotation.description: Annotation queues help you manage manual annotation/labeling for your LLM projects. Create queues, define annotation metrics, and track progress.
onboarding.datasets.value.improvement.de: Create datasets from production edge cases to improve your application
onboarding.datasets.value.predeploy.desc: Benchmark new releases before deploying to production
onboarding.datasets.value.structured.des: Run experiments on collections of inputs and expected outputs
onboarding.datasets.value.workflows.desc: Build custom workflows around your datasets via the API and SDKs, e.g. for fine-tuning, few-shotting
onboarding.datasets.title: Get Started with Datasets & Experiments
onboarding.datasets.description: Datasets in Langfuse are collections of inputs (and expected outputs) for your LLM application. You can run Experiments against these datasets to test new releases before deployment to production.
onboarding.scores.value.feedback.title: Collect user feedback
onboarding.scores.value.feedback.descrip: Gather thumbs up/down feedback from users to identify high and low quality outputs
onboarding.scores.value.model.title: Run model-based evaluations
onboarding.scores.value.model.descriptio: Use LLMs to automatically evaluate your application's outputs
onboarding.scores.value.quality.title: Track quality metrics
onboarding.scores.value.quality.descript: Monitor quality metrics over time to identify trends and issues
onboarding.scores.value.custom.title: Use custom metrics
onboarding.scores.value.custom.descripti: Langfuse's scores are flexible and can be used to track any metric that's associated with an LLM application
onboarding.scores.title: Get Started with Scores
onboarding.scores.description: Scores allow you to evaluate the quality/safety of your LLM application through user feedback, model-based evaluations, or manual review. Scores can be used programmatically via the API and SDKs to track custom metrics.
onboarding.sessions.title: You aren't using sessions yet
onboarding.sessions.description: Sessions let you group traces that belong to the same workflow, or conversation.
onboarding.sessions.instructions-before: To start using sessions, you need to add a
onboarding.users.title: You aren't tracking users yet
onboarding.users.description: Once you add a user ID to your traces, you can correlate costs, evaluations and other LLM Application metrics to better understand how they interact with your LLM applications.
onboarding.users.instructions-before: To start tracking users, you need to add a
onboarding.dataset-items.no-access: You don't have access to this feature, please contact your administrator
onboarding.dataset-items.title: Add items to your dataset
onboarding.dataset-items.description: Datasets are collections of specific edge cases and underrepresented patterns used to evaluate your application.
onboarding.dataset-items.upload-csv.desc: Import dataset items from a CSV file
onboarding.dataset-items.add-code.title: Add via Code
onboarding.dataset-items.add-code.descri: Use our Python/TS/JS SDKs or custom API
onboarding.dataset-items.select-traces.d: Creating items from production data is supported on single trace level. Click to view docs for more details.
